description: 12-turn conversation establishing engineering identity facts (role, language, IDE, tabs/spaces, retro name). Tests multi-fact identity recall.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: I'm a backend engineer on the payments team.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Noted — you're a backend engineer on payments.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: I write almost everything in Rust these days.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Got it — Rust is your primary language.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: I use Neovim as my editor, always have.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Understood — Neovim is your IDE of choice.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: And for the record: spaces, never tabs. Four spaces.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: We call our sprint retrospective the 'post-mortem' even when nothing broke. Team in-joke.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Got it — your team calls the sprint retrospective the 'post-mortem'.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: What's my role, my language, my editor, and what do we call the retro?

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: You're a backend engineer on payments, you primarily use Rust, your editor is Neovim, and your team calls the retrospective the 'post-mortem'.

gold_answers.role: Backend engineer on the payments team
